title: Lin Xue's Approach
content:
Chen Fan and Wang Tieshan returned to the Heavenly Sword Sect with the captured bandits. The Sect Master was pleased with their success, but Chen Fan couldn't shake the feeling that something was wrong.

He went to see Old Man Ye that night.

"You found something," Old Man Ye said, without looking up.

"One of the bandits was wearing the Heavenly Sword Sect symbol," Chen Fan said. "There's a traitor in the sect."

Old Man Ye nodded. "I knew this day would come. The corruption has been growing for years."

"What should I do?"

"Keep your eyes open," Old Man Ye said. "The traitor will reveal themselves in time. But for now, focus on your training. You need to get stronger if you want to face what's coming."

Chen Fan nodded and left. As he walked back to his room, he noticed a figure standing in the shadows.

"Chen Fan?"

It was Lin Xue.

"What are you doing here?" Chen Fan asked.

"I heard you came back from your mission," Lin Xue said. "I wanted to make sure you're okay."

"I'm fine," Chen Fan said.

Lin Xue smiled. "Good. I was worried about you."

Chen Fan felt his heart skip a beat. He quickly looked away.

"Thank you for your concern," he said.

"Chen Fan," Lin Xue said, stepping closer. "Can I ask you something?"

"What?"

"Why are you so distant?" Lin Xue asked. "Ever since we met, you've been keeping me at arm's length. Did I do something wrong?"

Chen Fan hesitated. Should he tell her the truth? About his past life, about her betrayal?

"No," he said. "You didn't do anything wrong. I'm just... not used to being around people."

Lin Xue looked at him, her eyes sad. "I see. Well, I'll leave you alone then."

She turned to walk away, but Chen Fan grabbed her arm.

"Wait," he said. "I'm sorry. It's not you. It's me. I have... issues."

Lin Xue smiled. "Then let me help you. I want to be your friend, Chen Fan. Truly."

Chen Fan looked into her eyes. He could see the sincerity in them, and for a moment, he forgot about his past life. He forgot about the betrayal, about the pain.

"Okay," he said. "We can try."

Lin Xue's smile widened, and she hugged him.

"Thank you," she said.

Chen Fan froze, his arms hanging at his sides. He couldn't bring himself to hug her back.

Finally, Lin Xue pulled away, her cheeks pink.

"I should go," she said. "Good night, Chen Fan."

She walked away, leaving Chen Fan standing alone in the moonlight.

He closed his eyes, trying to push the memories away. But they kept coming back - the forbidden chamber, the betrayal, the pain.

He knew he couldn't trust Lin Xue. Not yet. Not until he knew the truth.

***

The next day, Chen Fan went to the training yard to practice. As he was warming up, Zhou Tianming approached him.

"Heard you took down the Black Wind Gang," Zhou Tianming said. "Impressive."

"Thanks," Chen Fan said.

"But I'm sure you couldn't have done it alone," Zhou Tianming said. "Your friend Wang Tieshan must have helped."

Chen Fan frowned. "What's your point?"

"My point is," Zhou Tianming said, stepping closer, "that you're not as strong as everyone thinks. You just got lucky."

Chen Fan smiled. "If that's what you think, then let's fight. Right here, right now."

Zhou Tianming's eyes widened. "You're challenging me?"

"Yes," Chen Fan said. "Unless you're scared."

Zhou Tianming's face turned red. "Fine! Let's see who's stronger!"

They drew their swords and began to fight. The other disciples gathered around, cheering.

Zhou Tianming attacked first, his movements aggressive and desperate. But Chen Fan was too experienced. He dodged the attacks with ease, then countered with a series of strikes from the Heavenly Sword Art.

Within minutes, Zhou Tianming was on the ground, his sword knocked out of his hand.

"I yield," Zhou Tianming said, coughing.

Chen Fan walked away, leaving Zhou Tianming lying on the ground.

As he walked back to his room, he noticed Lin Xue watching him from the sidelines. She smiled and waved, and Chen Fan felt a strange warmth in his chest.

He quickly shook it off. He couldn't let his guard down.

But deep down, he knew that Lin Xue was different. This Lin Xue wasn't the same woman who had betrayed him in his past life.

Or was she?

He would have to wait and see.
